title: Fix return statements in PyMuPDFScraper
body:
Summary of changes:
- Add explicit return statements in the exception paths of PyMuPDFScraper.scrape, returning a stable 3-tuple of (text, image_list, title) instead of None. This aligns with the actual return shape and prevents unpacking errors in callers.
- Update the scrape method's return type annotation to reflect the actual return type: tuple[str, list[str], str].
- Update the docstring (implicitly via updated return type) to reflect the new, stable return format.

Rationale:
- The original implementation did not return in except blocks, causing the function to implicitly return None. Callers that unpack three values would fail.
- The method was annotated as returning str but actually returns a 3-tuple. This mismatch can lead to runtime errors and misinterpretation of the API surface.

Impact:
- Public API remains the same shape for successful loads (still returning (text, image, title)), but with robust error handling ensuring a consistent 3-tuple on failure as well. If external callers relied on a single string return before, they will need to adapt to the 3-tuple format.
- No other files are touched; changes are isolated to base_project/pymupdf.py.

step_plan.summary: This PR fixes the PyMuPDFScraper.scrape method to always return a stable 3-tuple (text, image_list, title) by adding explicit returns in exception paths and updating the return type annotation and docstring to reflect this API.

steps.operation: Update PyMuPDFScraper.scrape to return a consistent (text, image_list, title) tuple on all execution paths and update the function's type annotation and docstring
steps.reason: Previously, exception paths could return None and the function incorrectly declared a string return type; aligning all paths to a 3-tuple prevents unpacking errors and clarifies the API surface
steps.side_effects: Public API change requires downstream callers to unpack the 3-tuple; unit tests should cover success and error paths; may require updating code that consumed scrape() as a single string; potential impact on external integrations
